pageTitle.value: Steve Boyes' Great Spine of Africa - Perpetual Planet - Video

pageCaption.value: Explorer Steve Boyes and his team have proven that the Angolan Highlands are the source of the great Zambezi River.

WaPageTitle.value: Steve Boyes' Great Spine of Africa - Perpetual Planet - Video

metadescription.value: With Rolex's support, Steve Boyes is investigating the role of Africa’s major water stores as the sources of the continent's greatest rivers. Watch the video on rolex.org.

text.value: National Geographic Explorer Steve Boyes and his team have proven that the Angolan Highlands are the source of the great Zambezi River. With support from our Perpetual Planet Initiative, he has presented this world-first research to global leaders at a conference dedicated to the protection of wetlands. Now, the leader of the Great Spine of Africa series of expeditions is using his findings to secure greater protections for this vital area – taking him one step closer to safeguarding critical water sources.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Naturalist and professional diver Sandra Bessudo has worked for decades to conserve marine life around Malpelo Island, off the coast of Colombia.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

metadescription.value: As the Malpelo Island Hope Spot Champion, diver Sandra Bessudo leads expeditions to monitor the region twice a year. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Mission Blue: Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Hunters and fishermen almost eradicated Atlantic puffins from Maine’s offshore islands in the 19th century. The innovative techniques that 1987 Rolex Laureate Stephen W. Kress used to bring them back have inspired major changes in seabird conservation.

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes is revealing just how valuable the Angolan Highlands water tower is for the Congo River basin. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Lungwevungu River Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Venturing into the remote Angolan wilderness, conservationist and National Geographic Fellow Steve Boyes led an expedition to increase scientific knowledge of the mighty Zambezi River, on which millions of people depend. The discoveries they have made will help find ways to protect these vital waterways as the climate changes.

WaPageTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes and his team have set out to scientifically document the unexplored Lungwevungu River. Watch the video on rolex.org.
